name: catf
thesis: Assets with positive 12-month returns tend to continue rising short-term; assets with negative 12-month returns tend to continue falling. Time-series momentum is one of the most documented anomalies in finance, tested back to 1880 across every asset class.

revisions.change: maxDrawdown expected range 0.15-0.20 → 0.25-0.35; kill switch 0.25 → 0.40; graduation DD cap 0.25 → 0.35
revisions.reason: Original 20% DD target reflected vol-targeted professional managed-futures funds (AQR, Man AHL) that use vol-parity sizing and 40+ instruments. Our retail implementation is equal-weight across 7 assets without vol-targeting — literature supports 25-35% DD for this strategy class. 17-year backtest showed 38% DD in 2008 GFC. Category-error correction, not performance-based goalpost moving. Other criteria (PF, Sharpe, Z, CAGR, N) were unchanged and all passed.

Mandate originally assumed multi-strategy fund splitting a $20k paper bank. After 2026-04-14 strategy slate wipe, CATF is the only strategy on its own dedicated Alpaca paper account. Risk-manager syncEquity reads full broker equity ($20k), so mandate-driven startingEquity ($8k) was inconsistent with operational reality and broke dashboard P&L math. Category-error correction reflecting one-strategy-per-account architecture, not performance-based adjustment. Expected ranges, kill switches, and graduation criteria unchanged.
